stories.title: Johnny Depp Scrooges it up in Pearl director's dark Dickens adaptation — watch the trailer
stories.summary: Follow Empire on Google Just when you thought Hollywood had run out of ways to retell the tale of Charles Dickens' A Christmas Carol , along comes Pearl and Maxxxine director Ti West. And along comes Paramount Pictures' Ebenezer: A Christmas Carol . Yes, despite there already existing umpteen iterations of Dickens' festive ghost story — including, but not limited to, a CGI one . a Bill Murray one . a Muppet one , and an animated Nic Cage one — this Christmas we'll be seeing Johnny Depp go full Scrooge in West's star-studded, dark(er) take on the book.

stories.title: Love Island SPOILER: 'Gameplay' accusations rock the villa as Maya Jama puts Islanders in the hot seat
stories.summary: By on 23rd July 2026 in By on 23rd July 2026 in The events of The Grafties have boiled over into another episode of Love Island , as accusations of 'gameplay' have unsettled the Islanders. In a first look at Thursday's episode, Priya questions Julia over her thoughts on her connection with Aidan. She asks: 'Do you think I’m genuinely with Aidan as a gameplay, to get to the final? ' Julia admits: 'I do think you’ve developed a connection. He would pick you over any new bombshell as that would guarantee the final. ' Priya and Yasmin, Love Island. Pic: ITV The conversation shifts to the wider dynamics in the Villa.

stories.title: Maghnes Akliouche transfer news: Liverpool make initial approach for Monaco winger amid PSG interest
stories.summary:
Liverpool have made an initial approach to Monaco for winger Maghnes Akliouche.

Sky Sports News understands the club have checked on the potential availability of the 24-year-old France international.

  • Transfer Centre LIVE! | Liverpool news & transfers🔴

stories.title: [TUY8S2.2] Dust Storm Event, Creator Collaboration & Updates
stories.summary: CONTENTS • Dust Storm Event • Classified Assignment • Realism Mode • Creator Collaboration • Paradise Lost Update • Stats And Resources Multiplier Events • Changes and Fixes DUST STORM EVENT Summer is in full swing, and it’s time to experience New York as you have never seen it before. From July 21 to August 11 , defeat powerful enemies, collect exclusive loot and navigate hazardous weather conditions in the Dust Storm Event . All players who log in to the game during the event period will be granted the Parched Mask as a free reward. As part of the celebrations, access to the Warlords of New York expansion will be free for all players for the duration of the event.

stories.title: Saudi prince found dead in £600-a-night London hotel room as cause of death unearthed
stories.summary: A Saudi prince who was found dead in a lavish Kensington hotel room was almost three times over the drink-drive limit and had "fatal levels" of the party drug GHP in his system, an inquest heard. Abdullah bin Fahad bin Abdullah bin Abdulaziz bin Jalawi al Saud was found dead in his £600-a-night Marriott hotel room in Kensington on the 25 November last year. A cleaner found the prince, 29, lying in the bathroom of his locked fifth-floor room fully clothed. Hotel security and ambulance were called but were unable to resuscitate him . He was sadly pronounced dead at the scene.

stories.title: Miquita Oliver apologises to Fearne Cotton over past jibe
stories.summary: Miquita Oliver apologises to Fearne Cotton over 'awful' comment in the past Updated / Wednesday, 22 Jul 2026 18:15 Getty (L-R) Miquita Oliver and Fearne Cotton - Oliver was let go from her radio job in 2005 following an interview with NME where she was asked how she would improve Top of the Pops and replied that the programme should get rid of Cotton Presenter Miquita Oliver has apologised to Fearne Cotton after Oliver was sacked from BBC Radio 1 in 2005 for making an "awful" comment about her fellow broadcaster.

stories.title: US threatens 'major military punishment' for Houthi attacks
stories.summary: Skip next section US launches 13th night of strikes on Iran July 24, 2026 US launches 13th night of strikes on Iran US forces have said that they have conducted strikes "against Iranian military targets" for the 13th consecutive day. "This is the 13th consecutive night of strikes aimed to hold Iran accountable and diminish threats from the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps to commercial shipping," US Central Command (CENTCOM) said in a post on X. https://p. dw.

stories.title: Fortnite Cube Sprites Release Date Explained
stories.summary: What are the Fortnite Cube Sprites? This is a new feature that we’re expecting to arrive in the next Fortnite update. It’ll be a brand-new variant for each of the sprites. joining Holofoil, Gold, and Galaxy as a new type of collectible. But how exactly will they work? Chapter 7 Season 3 has gone all in on Sprites. A little collectible you can track down in each game. Giving you unique Sprite powers. But also, something to extract and collect, so you can use them in the cosmetics in the Battle Plus. Plus, the bragging rights of just having a really rare sprite. So how do the Fortnite Cube Sprites work and when is their release date going to be? What are Fortnite Cube Sprites?

stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: EU sanctions oust Russian president of world chess
stories.summary: Arkady Dvorkovich, the president of the International Chess Federation (FIDE) , said on Friday he ⁠was suspending his activity with the organisation after being placed on the EU list of sanctioned Russians . Dvorkovich, a former Russian deputy prime minister, complained that the designation was “unlawful and unfair”. A statement said Viswanathan Anand, an Indian world chess champion, would take over as the federation’s interim president. It came as the E⁠U has unveiled its largest sanctions package ​in four years against Russia, targeting 218 individuals ⁠and entities.

stories.title: Derry City get off lightly away to Rijeka in Conference League qualifier
stories.summary: Uefa Conference League: Rijeka 1 (Fruk 52) Derry City 0 Classy Croatian international Toni Fruk handed HNK Rijeka a slender victory over Derry City in the Uefa Conference League second-round qualifier at the Stadion Rujevica. The midfielder fired Rijeka ahead six minutes into the second-half with a left-footed finish from close range. Up to that point, the Candystripes had restricted Rijeka to long-range efforts, with neither goalkeeper troubled in the opening half.

stories.title: TV tonight: documentary reveals the shocking truth behind model family blogger Ruby Franke
stories.summary: The Devil in the Family: The Fall of Ruby Franke 9pm, BBC Two At its peak, Ruby Franke’s parenting YouTube channel was earning £100,000 a month. But, as this documentary series shows, her ostensibly model family was a house of cards. As her ex-husband puts it: “The devil knocked and we opened the door. ” Their downfall culminated in Franke’s arrest for child abuse in 2023 after her emaciated son escaped and summoned help. Thanks to the plentiful footage recorded by Franke herself, it’s easy to sense the roots of this horror. Phil Harrison The Chase Around the World 9pm, ITV1 The shrewd expansion of the Chase universe into a travel contest with a general knowledge quiz attached continues.

stories.title: Rosalía Thrills Costa Rican Fans in Bogotá with Song Dedication and National Flag
stories.summary: Rosalía delivered a heartwarming song dedication and national flag tribute during her concert in Bogotá, where she honored Costa Rican fans who traveled from Central America to see her perform live, highlighting the power of music to bring people together across borders.

stories.title: From Casting Rooms to Center Stage: Meet Maya Sharma, the Versatile Artist Joining Anupam Kher’s Jaane Pehchaane Anjane - jay-ho.com
stories.summary: Inside Jaane Pehchaane Anjane – The Artists Behind Anupam Kher’s New Musical Comedy-Drama | Part 2 From Delhi’s street theatre and Mumbai’s casting rooms to filmmaking, documentary work, Mithila art and now an international stage, Maya Sharma has spent more than two decades discovering storytelling from almost every side. This fall, that journey brings her to North America with Anupam Kher’s Jaane Pehchaane Anjane , including its Boston-area performance on October 11. When the curtain rises on Jaane Pehchaane Anjane , the immediate attention naturally falls on Anupam Kher and Swaroop Sampat.

stories.title: Trump scoffs at ‘affordability’ as he touts economic policies at Georgia rally
stories.summary: Donald Trump scoffed at the word “affordability” as a political message, describing it at a campaign rally on Wednesday as a term concocted by Democratic political consultants to criticize his presidency. Yet even as the president dismissed the notion of affordability, he devoted much of his speech at Wheeler high school to touting the bread-and-butter benefits of his economic policies. “So I just want you to remember that if you’re paying a little bit more – a lot of it’s come down now, we got it coming down very rapidly actually,” Trump said in Marietta, an Atlanta suburb, casting higher prices today as a lingering effect of the Biden administration.

stories.title: Simon Yam's daughter graduates with first-class honours, designs graduation gown
stories.summary: Hong Kong actor Simon Yam and supermodel Qi Qi's daughter, Ella Yam, has added another achievement to her growing list of accomplishments. The 21-year-old graduated with first-class honours from the London School of Economics and Political Science (LSE). On July 22, Yam shared a series of photos from her graduation ceremony on social media, with both of her proud parents by her side. Announcing the milestone, she wrote: "First class honours graduate! So grateful for this journey. Since day one I've always loved learning and graduating from my dream school has been an incredible experience.

stories.title: WNBA Spokesman Issues Statement After Sabrina Ionescu Denies Receiving WNBA 3-Point Contest Invite
stories.summary: It was hard to imagine that Sabrina Ionescu would not be on the list of contestants for this year’s WNBA All-Star 3-Point Contest. Which is exactly why so many quickly concluded she must have declined the invitation when her name was absent from the announcement. Ionescu herself, however, has since come out to directly challenge that assumption, stating that she cannot decline an offer she never officially received in the first place. But a WNBA spokesman has clarified the league’s process in such matters via ESPN’s Alexa Philippou.

stories.title: Video Global superstar Tyla on new album ‘A*Pop’: ‘I wanted to honor that inner child' - ABC News
stories.summary: Global superstar Tyla on new album ‘A*Pop’: ‘I wanted to honor that inner child' ABC News’ Rocsi Diaz sits down with Grammy Award-winning singer Tyla to discuss her new album “A*Pop,” genre-blending and the importance of representing her South African roots.
stories.sourceName: ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos
sources: ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos

stories.title: Massimo Bottura Turned A Modena Farmhouse Into One of Italy’s Most Coveted Stays
stories.summary: At Casa Maria Luigia, owners Massimo Bottura and Lara Gilmore welcome guests into their world of food, art, music, and community.

stories.title: Ajax Turner Senior Center board splits, takes conflicting votes, starts over
stories.summary: After a set of meetings in which two versions of the Senior Citizens Center Board took conflicting actions, the City of Clarksville provided an update on its plans. CLARKSVILLE, TN ( CLARKSVILLE NOW ) – After a set of meetings in which two versions of the Senior Citizens Center Board took conflicting actions, with resignations and restarts, the City of Clarksville provided an update on its plans, stating it will move forward with taking over the Ajax Turner Senior Center operations. But several questions are still unanswered. The City of Clarksville said Monday the Senior Citizens Center at 953 Clark St.

stories.title: Burnham to spend first day working from northern branch of No 10
stories.summary: Andy Burnham will hold his first meeting at the new No 10 North in Manchester today, marking a shift of government operations outside London. Up to 300 civil servants will relocate to the northern branch by the end of next year as part of plans to decentralise power. The prime minister will chair a National Economic Council meeting in Manchester, aiming to prioritise regional devolution and local investment.

stories.title: Creepy Twins, Religious Fanaticism and a Loaded Gun Collide in Tense Chamber Drama ‘Godhead’ — Fantasia
stories.summary: A rural Catholic priest is confronted by two unsettling twins who claim to be part of a prophecy and arrive armed with a gun. The tense standoff unfolds in Mark H. Rapaport’s black-and-white thriller “Godhead,” which premiered at Montreal’s Fantasia Festival this week. The film explores themes of faith, manipulation and authority through its unsettling narrative.

stories.title: Javier Bardem Reveals the Perks of Working With Wife Penelope Cruz: ‘There’s a Level of Trust’
stories.summary: Javier Bardem and Penélope Cruz will star as a married couple in the upcoming film Bunker, set for U.S. release in early 2027. The actors, who have been married since 2010, praised the trust and chemistry they bring to the project. Directed by Florian Zeller, the film follows an architect whose marriage is strained after he designs a billionaire’s bunker.

stories.title: ‘Stuart Fails to Save the Universe’ Co-Creator Bill Prady Explains the Science Behind the Show’s Multiverse
stories.summary: SPOILER ALERT: This story contains spoilers from the series premiere of “ Stuart Fails to Save the Universe ,” now streaming on HBO Max. HBO Max’s “Stuart Fails to Save the Universe” opens in a post-apocalyptic Pasadena, an alternative world where zombie hands poke out of the ground, giant moths terrorize, random tears in reality serve as vacuum portals to unknown parts of the galaxy and survivors barter with their last remaining canned goods. About the only thing still operating is the comic book shop run by Stuart Bloom (played by Kevin Sussman ), where the gang on “The Big Bang Theory” would sometimes hang out.
entities: Stuart Fails to Save the Universe

stories.title: US imposes tariffs on dozens of trade partners over 'forced labour' imports
stories.summary: The tariffs target around 60 trading partners over claims they failed to properly stop forced labour. The US is imposing new tariffs on around 60 trading partners accounting for the vast majority of its imports over claims they failed to properly stop forced labour. The duties, ranging from 10% to 12. 5%, target key economic partners – including the UK, China, EU, Canada, Japan and India. They come in on Friday, as a temporary 10% tax on foreign goods introduced earlier this year expires. The move is the latest escalation in the global trade war reignited by US President Donald Trump when he returned to office last year.

stories.title: Icon penniless after $60m ‘betrayal’
stories.summary: Former world No. 1 tennis player Arantxa Sanchez Vicario has revealed new details after her $60m fortune “vanished”. The four-time grand slam champ is still living a nightmare after she was handed a suspended two-year prison sentence and ordered to pay back $11 million worth of debts. The Australian Open finalist has given a tell-all interview with a Spanish TV show where she made new accusations against her ex-husband — the person she blames for her financial downfall. The 54-year-old was married to entrepreneur Josep Santacana between 2008 and 2019. They have two children. The former teenage sensation has turned on her former partner — blaming him entirely for the family’s financial ruin.

stories.title: Heatwave anxiety can make you feel 'incredibly helpless' - here's how to manage it
stories.summary: Extreme heat can take a toll on mental health - and young people and women are among the groups most at risk, according to one study. At the peak of the June heatwave, the warmest June on record in England, all Tavia Watts could think about was how hot it was in her two- and five-year-old's bedroom - above 30C in some cases. "During the heatwave, I've got a high level of anxiety all the time and I'm pretty much useless," she told the BBC. "I've had to leave bedtime when my husband reads [my kids] stories to have a little cry and to calm down, because it's just too hot in their room and I'm afraid for them.

stories.title: Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now?
stories.summary: At least 12 babies have been trafficked from Indonesia to Singapore, but what will happen to them remains unclear. Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now? Figure caption, Watch: A baby trafficking ring has been convicted - but what now for the babies? By Tessa Wong Reporting from Singapore and Astudestra Ajengrastri Reporting from Jakarta On Tuesday an Indonesian court convicted 19 people for baby trafficking, wrapping up a high-profile case - but leaving a key question unanswered. The members of the trafficking syndicate were found guilty of procuring at least 34 babies and selling them for adoption.

stories.title: Scientists find building blocks of birdsong's 'spectacular diversity'
stories.summary: Scientists in France say they have discovered the building blocks of the incredible diversity of birdsong. A team analysed the songs of more than 100,000 songbirds and revealed that the world's birdsongs are composed of eight fundamental types of sound that the researchers call "motifs". Birdsong all around the world, they say, is made up of combinations of these motifs - three types of trill, three types of whistle, chaotic notes and harmonies. Birdsong is one of the natural world's most sophisticated forms of sound-based communication. Its variety and complexity has fascinated biologists, but with so many different songs, it has been difficult to study on a global scale.

stories.title: China's Moonshot AI stole from Anthropic, Trump tech adviser says
stories.summary: The allegations come as Chinese AI companies are facing increased US government scrutiny. A White House adviser has accused China's Moonshot AI of a "large scale" effort to steal the capabilities of top US artificial intelligence (AI) models. US President Donald Trump's Science and Technology adviser Michael Kratsios said Moonshot AI carried out the campaign through what is known as distillation - when a weaker AI model extracts answers from a stronger one. Moonshot also gained access to restricted cutting-edge Nvidia servers to train its models, Kratsios said in a social post , external on Wednesday. The BBC has contacted Moonshot, Anthropic, the White House, and Nvidia for comment.

stories.title: Judge Extends Pause for Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
stories.summary: The court blocked the companies from closing the acquisition through mid-August, as a judge considers two lawsuits challenging it.

stories.title: What to Know About Peptides the FDA Is Considering for Use
stories.summary: The agency is considering whether compounding pharmacies should be allowed to produce BPC-157, TB-500 and other previously banned peptides.

stories.title: Littler cruises into Matchplay semis with win over Rock
stories.summary: World number one Luke Littler's World Matchplay title defence continues with a 16-7 quarter-final win over Josh Rock in Blackpool, as he averages 109. 57. Luke Littler's World Matchplay title defence continued with a straightforward 16-7 quarter-final win over Josh Rock in Blackpool. Rock initially kept pace with the world number one as the players traded breaks to reach 5-5. But after 19-year-old Littler reeled off five legs in a row, there proved to be no way back for Rock as the defending champion, in dazzling form, cruised into the semi-finals. After throwing 17 180s with a scoring average of 109. 57, Littler said: "At the minute, everything's going to plan.

stories.title: US health panel recommends easing restrictions on controversial peptides
stories.summary: A federal health advisory panel on Thursday narrowly voted 8-6 to ease restrictions on several peptides like BPC-157 and TB-500, despite safety concerns from government scientists. The FDA is not bound by the vote but may allow pharmacies to produce injectable peptides for wellness and cosmetic uses. Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr., a peptide advocate, has signaled plans to overturn existing FDA restrictions.

stories.title: Google fined €890m by EU for favouring its own apps over rivals
stories.summary: Google has been fined €890 million by the European Union for abusing its market dominance by favouring its own apps over rivals. The fine, the first under the EU’s Digital Markets Act, follows two breaches where Google gave its services an unfair advantage. Google warns the ruling could strip away key features for European users, but EU officials insist the penalties protect fair competition.

stories.title: King's Tardis entrance as Commonwealth Games begin
stories.summary: King Charles III emerges from a Tardis and a Loch Ness Monster takes the stage at the Commonwealth Games opening ceremony in Glasgow. One of the louder cheers came for the Falklands Islands – in the news recently following Argentina's victory over England in the football World Cup semi-finals – and another followed when the Samoa team emerged and performed a haka in front of the dignitaries in the Royal Box. But nothing compared to the noise which greeted the arrival of the Scotland team, who entered the arena last in their white shirts and kilts and lifted many in the capacity crowd from their seats.

stories.title: Kasper Dolberg reintegrated into Ajax squad as Celtic target’s exit stalls
stories.summary: Kasper Dolberg has been reintegrated into Ajax’s squad after being cut from their early pre-season friendlies. The striker, whose interest from Celtic was well-documented last summer, had seemingly been in limbo awaiting a move to Midtjylland. The Danish club confirmed talks with the striker, but these appear to have stalled for now. After featuring in their friendly against Olympiacos on Saturday, Dolberg has now been named in their travelling squad in Thursday’s Conference League qualifier against Vojvodina. Should Celtic try for Kasper Dolberg again? How much would you pay for him?

stories.title: Alan Hand becomes new President of Tullamore Lions Club
stories.summary: Dymphna Bracken and Alan Hand. Published: Thu 23 Jul 2026, 4:18 PM Alan Hand has been elected as the 52nd President of Tullamore Lions Club, succeeding outgoing president Dymphna Bracken. The official handover took place during the club’s annual changeover meeting at Giltraps of Kinnitty on Tuesday evening, where members gathered to mark the occasion and celebrate the club’s achievements over the past year. Alan previously served as the vice-president during the 25/26 term. Paying tribute to the outgoing president, the club thanked Dymphna for her commitment during her term. “We would like to thank Dymphna for her trojan work in the last 12 months,” the club said in a statement.

stories.title: How NEC Nijmegen could reach the UEFA Champions League for the first time
stories.summary: NEC Nijmegen had an unbelievable campaign that saw them reach the UEFA Champions League qualifiers for the first time. Check here who they'll face on their way to the league phase NEC Nijmegen 's third-place finish in the 2025/26 Eredivisie was the best in club history and earned them their first-ever spot in the UEFA Champions League qualifiers. The success is all self-made, engineered by manager Dick Schreuder, who rocked the Eredivisie with his aggressive attacking brand of football.

stories.title: Number of U.S. Troop Deaths in Iran War Appears Lower on Pentagon Website
stories.summary: Three military officials and a Pentagon spokesman gave conflicting explanations for why the number of Americans killed in the Iran war had changed.

stories.title: Murder of Thomas in Crépol: the court upholds the aggravating circumstance of racism – Interview
stories.summary: The investigating judge in Valence has reclassified the charges against the suspects in the murder of Thomas Perotto in Crépol. The magistrates have added an aggravating circumstance to the charges against the accused. This decision comes after lengthy debates that have characterized the investigation since its inception. A debated legal classification The aggravating circumstance cited relates to the fact that the victims were allegedly attacked because they were white and French citizens. This legal classification had been the subject of much debate since the beginning of the investigation. Thomas Perotto was killed in 2023 in an event that deeply shocked the public.

stories.title: Magnicity unveils reimagined euromast to boost rotterdams appeal for international travellers
stories.summary: Experiential 2 min Read Magnicity has reopened the renovated Euromast in Rotterdam following an extensive refurbishment. The transformed attraction introduces immersive storytelling, upgraded dining and enhanced observation experiences while targeting growing international markets, including India. The company aims to position the landmark as a key addition to premium leisure and MICE itineraries across the Netherlands. Magnicity has unveiled the newly renovated Euromast in Rotterdam, introducing a refreshed visitor experience that combines immersive attractions, upgraded hospitality and panoramic views of the Dutch city.

stories.title: FDA Panel Supports Lifting Restrictions on Four Peptides
stories.summary: In an all-day hearing, the agency’s scientists argued against allowing broad access to unproven drugs that the agency had banned three years ago.

stories.title: Amid Cyclospora Outbreak Tied to Lettuce, America Skips Salads
stories.summary: Foot traffic to chains such as Chopt and Chipotle fell in recent weeks after a recall from Taylor Farms and changing messages from the F.D.A.

stories.title: India activist ends hunger strike after 26 days
stories.summary: India’s prominent activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike after the government guaranteed no legal action against protesters at Delhi’s Jantar Mantar. The government ministers visited him in hospital as mass demonstrations continued following a violent police crackdown on Monday’s march to parliament. The youth-led CJP movement, now a major dissent force, vows to press on until Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan resigns.

stories.title: Microsoft responds to LG monitors installing McAfee ads on Windows
stories.summary: App is installed through Windows Update when certain LG monitors connect to a PC. Broader concerns Users have been finding LG Monitor App Installer and its ads on their systems without LG ever showing a prompt or asking for permission. LG has some of the most expensive computer monitors available. Paying, in some cases, over $1,000 for a monitor that ends up forcing ads onto Windows is disruptive and a privacy concern. Once the app is installed, “LG technically possesses permission to use ‘all system resources,’” as well as to “collect geolocation, device data, online activity, contacts, user credentials, transactions, and more,” Burke said.

stories.title: Hull KR inflict more derby misery on Hull FC
stories.summary: Hull KR beat Hull FC 28-20 in a tense Super League derby, ending their losing streak. Hull FC’s sixth straight defeat leaves them second-bottom as Hull KR climb to fifth in the play-off race. Willie Peters secured his ninth straight derby win as Hull KR coach in his final game.

stories.title: Health Care Agency Discontinues Dozens of Active Research Awards
stories.summary: The Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality slowed the awarding of new grants last year. Now it is stopping existing ones.
entities: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality

stories.title: Intel Benefits From a New Shift in A.I. Spending
stories.summary: The Silicon Valley chipmaker’s revenue rose 25 percent in the latest quarter, its fastest growth in 15 years, as A.I. firms increasingly bought chips known as central processing units.

stories.title: Key Republican Senator Says He Will Support Erica Schwartz for C.D.C. Director
stories.summary: Senator Bill Cassidy, who is believed to hold the deciding vote in the Senate health committee for Dr. Erica Schwartz’s confirmation, had declined for days to say whether he would back her.

offbeat.title: Ukraine war briefing: EU sanctions oust Russian president of world chess
offbeat.summary: Arkady Dvorkovich, the president of the International Chess Federation (FIDE) , said on Friday he ⁠was suspending his activity with the organisation after being placed on the EU list of sanctioned Russians . Dvorkovich, a former Russian deputy prime minister, complained that the designation was “unlawful and unfair”. A statement said Viswanathan Anand, an Indian world chess champion, would take over as the federation’s interim president. It came as the E⁠U has unveiled its largest sanctions package ​in four years against Russia, targeting 218 individuals ⁠and entities.

offbeat.title: Derry City get off lightly away to Rijeka in Conference League qualifier
offbeat.summary: Uefa Conference League: Rijeka 1 (Fruk 52) Derry City 0 Classy Croatian international Toni Fruk handed HNK Rijeka a slender victory over Derry City in the Uefa Conference League second-round qualifier at the Stadion Rujevica. The midfielder fired Rijeka ahead six minutes into the second-half with a left-footed finish from close range. Up to that point, the Candystripes had restricted Rijeka to long-range efforts, with neither goalkeeper troubled in the opening half.

offbeat.title: TV tonight: documentary reveals the shocking truth behind model family blogger Ruby Franke
offbeat.summary: The Devil in the Family: The Fall of Ruby Franke 9pm, BBC Two At its peak, Ruby Franke’s parenting YouTube channel was earning £100,000 a month. But, as this documentary series shows, her ostensibly model family was a house of cards. As her ex-husband puts it: “The devil knocked and we opened the door. ” Their downfall culminated in Franke’s arrest for child abuse in 2023 after her emaciated son escaped and summoned help. Thanks to the plentiful footage recorded by Franke herself, it’s easy to sense the roots of this horror. Phil Harrison The Chase Around the World 9pm, ITV1 The shrewd expansion of the Chase universe into a travel contest with a general knowledge quiz attached continues.

offbeat.title: Rosalía Thrills Costa Rican Fans in Bogotá with Song Dedication and National Flag
offbeat.summary: Rosalía delivered a heartwarming song dedication and national flag tribute during her concert in Bogotá, where she honored Costa Rican fans who traveled from Central America to see her perform live, highlighting the power of music to bring people together across borders.

offbeat.title: From Casting Rooms to Center Stage: Meet Maya Sharma, the Versatile Artist Joining Anupam Kher’s Jaane Pehchaane Anjane - jay-ho.com
offbeat.summary: Inside Jaane Pehchaane Anjane – The Artists Behind Anupam Kher’s New Musical Comedy-Drama | Part 2 From Delhi’s street theatre and Mumbai’s casting rooms to filmmaking, documentary work, Mithila art and now an international stage, Maya Sharma has spent more than two decades discovering storytelling from almost every side. This fall, that journey brings her to North America with Anupam Kher’s Jaane Pehchaane Anjane , including its Boston-area performance on October 11. When the curtain rises on Jaane Pehchaane Anjane , the immediate attention naturally falls on Anupam Kher and Swaroop Sampat.

offbeat.title: Trump scoffs at ‘affordability’ as he touts economic policies at Georgia rally
offbeat.summary: Donald Trump scoffed at the word “affordability” as a political message, describing it at a campaign rally on Wednesday as a term concocted by Democratic political consultants to criticize his presidency. Yet even as the president dismissed the notion of affordability, he devoted much of his speech at Wheeler high school to touting the bread-and-butter benefits of his economic policies. “So I just want you to remember that if you’re paying a little bit more – a lot of it’s come down now, we got it coming down very rapidly actually,” Trump said in Marietta, an Atlanta suburb, casting higher prices today as a lingering effect of the Biden administration.

offbeat.title: Simon Yam's daughter graduates with first-class honours, designs graduation gown
offbeat.summary: Hong Kong actor Simon Yam and supermodel Qi Qi's daughter, Ella Yam, has added another achievement to her growing list of accomplishments. The 21-year-old graduated with first-class honours from the London School of Economics and Political Science (LSE). On July 22, Yam shared a series of photos from her graduation ceremony on social media, with both of her proud parents by her side. Announcing the milestone, she wrote: "First class honours graduate! So grateful for this journey. Since day one I've always loved learning and graduating from my dream school has been an incredible experience.

offbeat.title: WNBA Spokesman Issues Statement After Sabrina Ionescu Denies Receiving WNBA 3-Point Contest Invite
offbeat.summary: It was hard to imagine that Sabrina Ionescu would not be on the list of contestants for this year’s WNBA All-Star 3-Point Contest. Which is exactly why so many quickly concluded she must have declined the invitation when her name was absent from the announcement. Ionescu herself, however, has since come out to directly challenge that assumption, stating that she cannot decline an offer she never officially received in the first place. But a WNBA spokesman has clarified the league’s process in such matters via ESPN’s Alexa Philippou.

offbeat.title: Video Global superstar Tyla on new album ‘A*Pop’: ‘I wanted to honor that inner child' - ABC News
offbeat.summary: Global superstar Tyla on new album ‘A*Pop’: ‘I wanted to honor that inner child' ABC News’ Rocsi Diaz sits down with Grammy Award-winning singer Tyla to discuss her new album “A*Pop,” genre-blending and the importance of representing her South African roots.
offbeat.sourceName: ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos
sources: ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos

offbeat.title: Massimo Bottura Turned A Modena Farmhouse Into One of Italy’s Most Coveted Stays
offbeat.summary: At Casa Maria Luigia, owners Massimo Bottura and Lara Gilmore welcome guests into their world of food, art, music, and community.

offbeat.title: Ajax Turner Senior Center board splits, takes conflicting votes, starts over
offbeat.summary: After a set of meetings in which two versions of the Senior Citizens Center Board took conflicting actions, the City of Clarksville provided an update on its plans. CLARKSVILLE, TN ( CLARKSVILLE NOW ) – After a set of meetings in which two versions of the Senior Citizens Center Board took conflicting actions, with resignations and restarts, the City of Clarksville provided an update on its plans, stating it will move forward with taking over the Ajax Turner Senior Center operations. But several questions are still unanswered. The City of Clarksville said Monday the Senior Citizens Center at 953 Clark St.

offbeat.title: Javier Bardem Reveals the Perks of Working With Wife Penelope Cruz: ‘There’s a Level of Trust’
offbeat.summary: Javier Bardem and Penélope Cruz will star as a married couple in the upcoming film Bunker, set for U.S. release in early 2027. The actors, who have been married since 2010, praised the trust and chemistry they bring to the project. Directed by Florian Zeller, the film follows an architect whose marriage is strained after he designs a billionaire’s bunker.

offbeat.title: Icon penniless after $60m ‘betrayal’
offbeat.summary: Former world No. 1 tennis player Arantxa Sanchez Vicario has revealed new details after her $60m fortune “vanished”. The four-time grand slam champ is still living a nightmare after she was handed a suspended two-year prison sentence and ordered to pay back $11 million worth of debts. The Australian Open finalist has given a tell-all interview with a Spanish TV show where she made new accusations against her ex-husband — the person she blames for her financial downfall. The 54-year-old was married to entrepreneur Josep Santacana between 2008 and 2019. They have two children. The former teenage sensation has turned on her former partner — blaming him entirely for the family’s financial ruin.

offbeat.title: Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now?
offbeat.summary: At least 12 babies have been trafficked from Indonesia to Singapore, but what will happen to them remains unclear. Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now? Figure caption, Watch: A baby trafficking ring has been convicted - but what now for the babies? By Tessa Wong Reporting from Singapore and Astudestra Ajengrastri Reporting from Jakarta On Tuesday an Indonesian court convicted 19 people for baby trafficking, wrapping up a high-profile case - but leaving a key question unanswered. The members of the trafficking syndicate were found guilty of procuring at least 34 babies and selling them for adoption.

offbeat.title: Kasper Dolberg reintegrated into Ajax squad as Celtic target’s exit stalls
offbeat.summary: Kasper Dolberg has been reintegrated into Ajax’s squad after being cut from their early pre-season friendlies. The striker, whose interest from Celtic was well-documented last summer, had seemingly been in limbo awaiting a move to Midtjylland. The Danish club confirmed talks with the striker, but these appear to have stalled for now. After featuring in their friendly against Olympiacos on Saturday, Dolberg has now been named in their travelling squad in Thursday’s Conference League qualifier against Vojvodina. Should Celtic try for Kasper Dolberg again? How much would you pay for him?

offbeat.title: Alan Hand becomes new President of Tullamore Lions Club
offbeat.summary: Dymphna Bracken and Alan Hand. Published: Thu 23 Jul 2026, 4:18 PM Alan Hand has been elected as the 52nd President of Tullamore Lions Club, succeeding outgoing president Dymphna Bracken. The official handover took place during the club’s annual changeover meeting at Giltraps of Kinnitty on Tuesday evening, where members gathered to mark the occasion and celebrate the club’s achievements over the past year. Alan previously served as the vice-president during the 25/26 term. Paying tribute to the outgoing president, the club thanked Dymphna for her commitment during her term. “We would like to thank Dymphna for her trojan work in the last 12 months,” the club said in a statement.

offbeat.title: How NEC Nijmegen could reach the UEFA Champions League for the first time
offbeat.summary: NEC Nijmegen had an unbelievable campaign that saw them reach the UEFA Champions League qualifiers for the first time. Check here who they'll face on their way to the league phase NEC Nijmegen 's third-place finish in the 2025/26 Eredivisie was the best in club history and earned them their first-ever spot in the UEFA Champions League qualifiers. The success is all self-made, engineered by manager Dick Schreuder, who rocked the Eredivisie with his aggressive attacking brand of football.

offbeat.title: Murder of Thomas in Crépol: the court upholds the aggravating circumstance of racism – Interview
offbeat.summary: The investigating judge in Valence has reclassified the charges against the suspects in the murder of Thomas Perotto in Crépol. The magistrates have added an aggravating circumstance to the charges against the accused. This decision comes after lengthy debates that have characterized the investigation since its inception. A debated legal classification The aggravating circumstance cited relates to the fact that the victims were allegedly attacked because they were white and French citizens. This legal classification had been the subject of much debate since the beginning of the investigation. Thomas Perotto was killed in 2023 in an event that deeply shocked the public.

offbeat.title: Magnicity unveils reimagined euromast to boost rotterdams appeal for international travellers
offbeat.summary: Experiential 2 min Read Magnicity has reopened the renovated Euromast in Rotterdam following an extensive refurbishment. The transformed attraction introduces immersive storytelling, upgraded dining and enhanced observation experiences while targeting growing international markets, including India. The company aims to position the landmark as a key addition to premium leisure and MICE itineraries across the Netherlands. Magnicity has unveiled the newly renovated Euromast in Rotterdam, introducing a refreshed visitor experience that combines immersive attractions, upgraded hospitality and panoramic views of the Dutch city.

offbeat.title: India activist ends hunger strike after 26 days
offbeat.summary: India’s prominent activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike after the government guaranteed no legal action against protesters at Delhi’s Jantar Mantar. The government ministers visited him in hospital as mass demonstrations continued following a violent police crackdown on Monday’s march to parliament. The youth-led CJP movement, now a major dissent force, vows to press on until Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan resigns.

offbeat.title: Microsoft responds to LG monitors installing McAfee ads on Windows
offbeat.summary: App is installed through Windows Update when certain LG monitors connect to a PC. Broader concerns Users have been finding LG Monitor App Installer and its ads on their systems without LG ever showing a prompt or asking for permission. LG has some of the most expensive computer monitors available. Paying, in some cases, over $1,000 for a monitor that ends up forcing ads onto Windows is disruptive and a privacy concern. Once the app is installed, “LG technically possesses permission to use ‘all system resources,’” as well as to “collect geolocation, device data, online activity, contacts, user credentials, transactions, and more,” Burke said.

politics.title: Burnham to spend first day working from northern branch of No 10
politics.summary: Andy Burnham will hold his first meeting at the new No 10 North in Manchester today, marking a shift of government operations outside London. Up to 300 civil servants will relocate to the northern branch by the end of next year as part of plans to decentralise power. The prime minister will chair a National Economic Council meeting in Manchester, aiming to prioritise regional devolution and local investment.

politics.title: Number of U.S. Troop Deaths in Iran War Appears Lower on Pentagon Website
politics.summary: Three military officials and a Pentagon spokesman gave conflicting explanations for why the number of Americans killed in the Iran war had changed.

politics.title: FDA Panel Supports Lifting Restrictions on Four Peptides
politics.summary: In an all-day hearing, the agency’s scientists argued against allowing broad access to unproven drugs that the agency had banned three years ago.

entertainment.title: Creepy Twins, Religious Fanaticism and a Loaded Gun Collide in Tense Chamber Drama ‘Godhead’ — Fantasia
entertainment.summary: A rural Catholic priest is confronted by two unsettling twins who claim to be part of a prophecy and arrive armed with a gun. The tense standoff unfolds in Mark H. Rapaport’s black-and-white thriller “Godhead,” which premiered at Montreal’s Fantasia Festival this week. The film explores themes of faith, manipulation and authority through its unsettling narrative.

entertainment.title: ‘Stuart Fails to Save the Universe’ Co-Creator Bill Prady Explains the Science Behind the Show’s Multiverse
entertainment.summary: SPOILER ALERT: This story contains spoilers from the series premiere of “ Stuart Fails to Save the Universe ,” now streaming on HBO Max. HBO Max’s “Stuart Fails to Save the Universe” opens in a post-apocalyptic Pasadena, an alternative world where zombie hands poke out of the ground, giant moths terrorize, random tears in reality serve as vacuum portals to unknown parts of the galaxy and survivors barter with their last remaining canned goods. About the only thing still operating is the comic book shop run by Stuart Bloom (played by Kevin Sussman ), where the gang on “The Big Bang Theory” would sometimes hang out.
entities: Stuart Fails to Save the Universe

breaking.title: US imposes tariffs on dozens of trade partners over 'forced labour' imports
breaking.summary: The tariffs target around 60 trading partners over claims they failed to properly stop forced labour. The US is imposing new tariffs on around 60 trading partners accounting for the vast majority of its imports over claims they failed to properly stop forced labour. The duties, ranging from 10% to 12. 5%, target key economic partners – including the UK, China, EU, Canada, Japan and India. They come in on Friday, as a temporary 10% tax on foreign goods introduced earlier this year expires. The move is the latest escalation in the global trade war reignited by US President Donald Trump when he returned to office last year.

facts.title: Heatwave anxiety can make you feel 'incredibly helpless' - here's how to manage it
facts.summary: Extreme heat can take a toll on mental health - and young people and women are among the groups most at risk, according to one study. At the peak of the June heatwave, the warmest June on record in England, all Tavia Watts could think about was how hot it was in her two- and five-year-old's bedroom - above 30C in some cases. "During the heatwave, I've got a high level of anxiety all the time and I'm pretty much useless," she told the BBC. "I've had to leave bedtime when my husband reads [my kids] stories to have a little cry and to calm down, because it's just too hot in their room and I'm afraid for them.

facts.title: Scientists find building blocks of birdsong's 'spectacular diversity'
facts.summary: Scientists in France say they have discovered the building blocks of the incredible diversity of birdsong. A team analysed the songs of more than 100,000 songbirds and revealed that the world's birdsongs are composed of eight fundamental types of sound that the researchers call "motifs". Birdsong all around the world, they say, is made up of combinations of these motifs - three types of trill, three types of whistle, chaotic notes and harmonies. Birdsong is one of the natural world's most sophisticated forms of sound-based communication. Its variety and complexity has fascinated biologists, but with so many different songs, it has been difficult to study on a global scale.

facts.title: What to Know About Peptides the FDA Is Considering for Use
facts.summary: The agency is considering whether compounding pharmacies should be allowed to produce BPC-157, TB-500 and other previously banned peptides.

crisis.title: China's Moonshot AI stole from Anthropic, Trump tech adviser says
crisis.summary: The allegations come as Chinese AI companies are facing increased US government scrutiny. A White House adviser has accused China's Moonshot AI of a "large scale" effort to steal the capabilities of top US artificial intelligence (AI) models. US President Donald Trump's Science and Technology adviser Michael Kratsios said Moonshot AI carried out the campaign through what is known as distillation - when a weaker AI model extracts answers from a stronger one. Moonshot also gained access to restricted cutting-edge Nvidia servers to train its models, Kratsios said in a social post , external on Wednesday. The BBC has contacted Moonshot, Anthropic, the White House, and Nvidia for comment.

crisis.title: Amid Cyclospora Outbreak Tied to Lettuce, America Skips Salads
crisis.summary: Foot traffic to chains such as Chopt and Chipotle fell in recent weeks after a recall from Taylor Farms and changing messages from the F.D.A.

tech.title: Judge Extends Pause for Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
tech.summary: The court blocked the companies from closing the acquisition through mid-August, as a judge considers two lawsuits challenging it.

tech.title: Google fined €890m by EU for favouring its own apps over rivals
tech.summary: Google has been fined €890 million by the European Union for abusing its market dominance by favouring its own apps over rivals. The fine, the first under the EU’s Digital Markets Act, follows two breaches where Google gave its services an unfair advantage. Google warns the ruling could strip away key features for European users, but EU officials insist the penalties protect fair competition.

tech.title: Intel Benefits From a New Shift in A.I. Spending
tech.summary: The Silicon Valley chipmaker’s revenue rose 25 percent in the latest quarter, its fastest growth in 15 years, as A.I. firms increasingly bought chips known as central processing units.

sports.title: Littler cruises into Matchplay semis with win over Rock
sports.summary: World number one Luke Littler's World Matchplay title defence continues with a 16-7 quarter-final win over Josh Rock in Blackpool, as he averages 109. 57. Luke Littler's World Matchplay title defence continued with a straightforward 16-7 quarter-final win over Josh Rock in Blackpool. Rock initially kept pace with the world number one as the players traded breaks to reach 5-5. But after 19-year-old Littler reeled off five legs in a row, there proved to be no way back for Rock as the defending champion, in dazzling form, cruised into the semi-finals. After throwing 17 180s with a scoring average of 109. 57, Littler said: "At the minute, everything's going to plan.

sports.title: King's Tardis entrance as Commonwealth Games begin
sports.summary: King Charles III emerges from a Tardis and a Loch Ness Monster takes the stage at the Commonwealth Games opening ceremony in Glasgow. One of the louder cheers came for the Falklands Islands – in the news recently following Argentina's victory over England in the football World Cup semi-finals – and another followed when the Samoa team emerged and performed a haka in front of the dignitaries in the Royal Box. But nothing compared to the noise which greeted the arrival of the Scotland team, who entered the arena last in their white shirts and kilts and lifted many in the capacity crowd from their seats.

sports.title: Hull KR inflict more derby misery on Hull FC
sports.summary: Hull KR beat Hull FC 28-20 in a tense Super League derby, ending their losing streak. Hull FC’s sixth straight defeat leaves them second-bottom as Hull KR climb to fifth in the play-off race. Willie Peters secured his ninth straight derby win as Hull KR coach in his final game.

health.title: US health panel recommends easing restrictions on controversial peptides
health.summary: A federal health advisory panel on Thursday narrowly voted 8-6 to ease restrictions on several peptides like BPC-157 and TB-500, despite safety concerns from government scientists. The FDA is not bound by the vote but may allow pharmacies to produce injectable peptides for wellness and cosmetic uses. Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr., a peptide advocate, has signaled plans to overturn existing FDA restrictions.

health.title: Health Care Agency Discontinues Dozens of Active Research Awards
health.summary: The Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality slowed the awarding of new grants last year. Now it is stopping existing ones.
entities: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality

health.title: Key Republican Senator Says He Will Support Erica Schwartz for C.D.C. Director
health.summary: Senator Bill Cassidy, who is believed to hold the deciding vote in the Senate health committee for Dr. Erica Schwartz’s confirmation, had declined for days to say whether he would back her.
